channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: New York lawmakers pass one-year ban on new data centers
item.description: The New York State legislature passed a one-year moratorium on new large data centers, the first statewide ban of its kind if Democratic Governor Kathy Hochul signs it into law. Lawmakers behind...

item.title: How We Built Private Agent Payments on Solana with Zero-Knowledge Proofs
item.description: The AI agent economy is scaling fast. Agents pay for APIs, buy data from other agents, settle trades, and manage resources autonomously. But running these agents on public blockchains introduces a...

item.title: Stop Uploading Your App Manually — Let Fastlane Do It For You
item.description: Every mobile dev has a release day ritual. Mine was eating 30–40 minutes a week — and adding zero value to anyone. You know the drill. Open Play Console, create a release, upload the AAB, write...

item.title: Czech ARES registry: what the official data actually returns
item.description: Eight digits identify almost every Czech company you will encounter in a registry search. The number is the IČO. It is assigned at incorporation and almost never changes during the entity’s...

item.title: Stop Building AI Features Like This — MCP Changes the Game
item.description: AI is everywhere in apps now: chatbots copilots smart search automation But most developers are still building AI features like this: 👉 send prompt → get response → repeat It works… Until it...

item.title: The Mental Model That Unlocks All of Go
item.description: You want to learn Go? Really learn it, not just copy-paste from Stack Overflow? Forget syntax. Forget keywords. Forget the "right way" to format errors. There's one mental model that, once...

item.title: Running AI Locally: Skip the API Bills and Build Faster
item.description: Running AI Locally: Skip the API Bills and Build Faster Your coding session just started. You need to refactor a gnarly function, write tests, or debug something weird. Do you really want to fire...

item.title: Astronauts on ISS told to shelter as repairs under way to fix air leaks
item.description: Article URL: https://www.bbc.com/news/live/c4g44ew3g1kt Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48413464 Points: 52 # Comments: 8

item.title: Newton-Raphson Method
item.description: ALGORITHM: Newton-Raphson Method for Finding Roots INPUT: - Function f(x) - Derivative f'(x) - Initial guess x₀ - Tolerance ε (epsilon) - Maximum iterations N OUTPUT: - Approximate root xₙ -...

item.title: Clone Yourself in 15 Minutes — But On Whose Terms?
item.description: Lenny Rachitsky's newsletter just walked operators through cloning themselves with Gemini Omni in under 15 minutes. Voice, face, knowledge base, response patterns. Fifteen minutes. The tools are...

item.title: Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
item.description: Microsoft’s AI products aren’t selling and Github’s been plagued with troubles. WIRED spoke with VP Scott Hanselman about whether the company is in catch-up mode.

item.title: Dev Opportunity Radar #2: A Fully-Funded Residency in Finland, AI Research Program, and a $60K Hackathon
item.description: TL;DR Welcome back to Dev Opportunity Radar. This is a weekly series where I share opportunities, resources, and interesting finds that I come across, with the goal of helping people discover...

item.title: The token bill comes due: Inside the industry scramble to manage AI’s runaway costs
item.description: "The whole conversation shifted from tokenmaxxing and 'go fast' to 'we need guardrails, how do we control this?'"

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in May 2026
item.description: May AI recap

item.title: MAHA wants to make cotton the new beef tallow
item.description: In between beef tallow fries, raw milk, and vaccine denialism, Make America Healthy Again figureheads have set their sights on another slice of life: our clothing. "The MAHA movement doesn't stop...

item.title: What do you mean my new smart scale is ‘built for GLP-1 users’?
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: This AI startup says it can tell if a script will make a hit film
item.description: When Quilty hit the industry trades earlier this year, the AI startup promised that its tool could accurately predict a film's success just by reading the script. When people actually got a chance...

item.title: Why isn’t the Trump phone made in the USA?
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This week, I'm investigating where it might have...

item.title: AI Worm
item.description: Researchers have prototyped an AI-powered internet worm. The coolest thing about the prototype is that it carries its own LLM with it, and runs it on computers that have been broken into. This is...

item.title: AirTrunk commits $30B to build 5GW of AI data centers in India
item.description: The Australian data center operator plans to set up 5GW of capacity in India.

item.title: This chunky little tablet got my kid to clean up his toys
item.description: Never underestimate the power that a cheap tablet holds over a kid under six. The Skylight Buddy is a device with one job: to be a cute little guy that helps your kid track routines and chores....

item.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 (where "OP" stands for "opponent") that has been observed targeting Microsoft Internet Information...

item.title: The Download: AI hacking beyond Mythos, and chatbots’ impact on our brains
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Meta hack shows there’s more to AI security than...

item.title: I customized a MacBook Neo with colorful spare parts
item.description: The MacBook Neo is Apple's cheapest laptop, its most colorful, and its easiest to repair in years. That means owners can buy replacement parts in all four of its available colors and swap them in...

item.title: Only 10% of SOCs Say They’re Getting Excellent Value From AI. Here’s What the Second Wave Has to Deliver
item.description: Eighteen months ago, the AI SOC was a marketing line. Today it's a budget item. The category has crossed over from interesting to inevitable, with billions of dollars now flowing into AI-powered...

item.title: Porsche’s Cayenne Coupe Turbo will even make 911 owners nervous
item.description: Back in 2002, Porsche fans sputtered with rage as the Cayenne made its debut at the Paris Motor. More than 20 years later, Porsche now sells more SUVs than anything else in its lineup. Last year,...

item.title: OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides
item.description: “Why wouldn’t you want to be in both Pepsi and Coke?” says one venture capitalist. “It’s the same here.”

item.title: Why Apple Might Put Cameras Into Its Next AirPods
item.description: From battery life to privacy, there are many hurdles to the idea taking off.

item.title: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
item.description: This week I’ve been at SXSW London. There’s been music, film, and a lot—and I mean a lot—of talk about AI. I also had the opportunity to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University...

item.title: The Meta hack shows there’s more to AI security than Mythos
item.description: On June 5, 404 Media reported that attackers had been using Meta’s AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. Their approach was simple: They asked the agent to link the accounts to...
